meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Portfolio (Beginner)
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing to overcome the no-portfolio trap. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first app.
content.answer_block: The 'no-portfolio' trap is solved by shipping a functional SaaS product with integrated pricing. By moving from static code to a monetized architecture, you demonstrate real-world engineering capability. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement to build a professional-grade project that proves your hireability to recruiters.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a GitHub profile is the silent killer of tech careers. You’ve mastered the syntax, but you lack the battle scars of a production-ready application. Without a real product that handles users, payments, and data, you remain invisible in a sea of generic tutorials. It is time to stop building 'to-do lists' and start building a business.

Today, we bridge the gap between student and developer. By implementing a SaaS pricing model—Stripe integration, tier management, and access control—you turn a hobby project into a professional asset. This isn't just about code; it's about proving you can handle the complexities that keep senior engineers awake at night.

steps.title: Define Your Value Architecture
steps.description: Before coding, map out your pricing tiers. Define what features are free versus premium to show you understand product-led growth.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a SaaS pricing model schema for a task management app with three tiers: Free, Pro ($10/mo), and Team ($29/mo). Include feature flags for each tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate the database schema for a multi-tier SaaS pricing model using Prisma.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Payment Gateway
steps.description: Integrate Stripe to handle subscriptions. This proves you can work with secure third-party APIs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe webhook handler in Next.js that listens for 'checkout.session.completed' and updates the user's subscription status in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add stripe-node --prompt 'Create a secure checkout session endpoint.'

steps.title: Implement Access Control Middleware
steps.description: Secure your routes based on the user's subscription status to prevent unauthorized access to premium features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that checks if the user's subscription plan allows access to the 'analytics' dashboard route.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement Next.js middleware to protect routes based on user subscription tier.'

steps.title: Build the Pricing UI
steps.description: Create a high-converting pricing table that dynamically updates based on your application state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a responsive pricing table component in Tailwind CSS that highlights the 'Pro' plan and fetches current pricing from the Stripe API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add lucide-react --prompt 'Build a pricing table component with toggleable monthly/yearly billing.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Push your code to production and verify that your payment flow functions in test mode.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test script that simulates a user subscribing to the Pro plan and verifies the database status update.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Configure Vercel deployment and run end-to-end subscription tests.'

pro_tips: Always store prices in cents, not dollars, to avoid floating-point math errors in your production database.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your backend to avoid immediate service cutoff when payments fail.
Use feature flags instead of hard-coding UI elements to make your pricing tiers easily adjustable without redeploying.
Always include a 'Cancel Subscription' flow that collects user feedback; recruiters love seeing data-driven retention logic.
Set up Stripe Webhooks locally using the Stripe CLI to test real-world scenarios before deploying to production.
content.next_steps: After deploying, add a 'Feedback' loop to your SaaS. Tracking how users interact with your pricing page is the next step in becoming a full-stack engineer.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder with a live, monetized application on your portfolio. This project is the key to unlocking your first professional role. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
